title: Already-updated goal plugin hook reference still omits live cleanup env vars and output fields
claim: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/hooks/goal_plugin.md was updated for goal_opencode.md but still omits MK_GOAL_STATE_ARCHIVE_RETENTION_DAYS, MK_GOAL_STATE_ACTIVE_RETENTION_DAYS, MK_GOAL_STATE_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS, store_health, and mutation= coverage.
counterevidenceSought: Read the full goal_plugin.md file from lines 1-83, including frontmatter, behavior contract, environment table, boundaries, verification, and related references; no later section documents the missing env vars or output fields.
alternativeExplanation: The file may have been updated only for the command filename, but its own purpose statement says it names command boundary, environment controls, and validation surfaces, so missing live env/output behavior remains a traceability gap.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if goal_plugin.md is explicitly scoped down to a quick-start and links to another authoritative operator contract that documents these env vars and output fields.
scopeProof: The hook reference and central ENV_REFERENCE sibling were both checked against mk-goal.js source-of-truth definitions/emission paths.

claim: ENV_REFERENCE.md and goal_plugin.md both omit the three MK_GOAL_STATE_* env vars, so there are no documented sibling naming/default rows to compare; both remain inconsistent with mk-goal.js.
